AlertCode.description: Machine-readable reason code for an import alert.
AlertCode.title: AlertCode

ChangedEntry.description:
An existing location whose fields differ from the import row.

Each field is either the plain new value (unchanged) or a ChangedField object
carrying both new and old values.
ChangedEntry.title: ChangedEntry

DeliveryTypeEnum.description:
Kind of recipient at a Location — set per location, enforced uniform
within a RouteGroup at generation time.
DeliveryTypeEnum.title: DeliveryTypeEnum

DriverAssignmentStatusEnum.description:
Whether the routes in a RouteGroup have drivers assigned (read from
Route.driver_id).
DriverAssignmentStatusEnum.title: DriverAssignmentStatusEnum

DriverHistorySummary.description: Summary of driver's lifetime and current year kilometers
DriverHistorySummary.title: DriverHistorySummary

DriverHistoryUpdate.description: Update request model, all fields are required for now since we are only updating km
DriverHistoryUpdate.title: DriverHistoryUpdate

DriverRegisterResponse.description: Driver registration response - contains Driver object and AuthResponse
DriverRegisterResponse.title: DriverRegisterResponse

LocationGroupUpdate.description: Location group update request - all optional
LocationGroupUpdate.title: LocationGroupUpdate

LocationImportEntry.description: Parsed row from import file; all fields optional until validated.
LocationImportEntry.title: LocationImportEntry

LocationImportResponse.description:
Combined validate + review-changes payload.

success=False when any row has alerts. net_new/stale/changed describe how the
import would affect the existing locations table; these are placeholders until
the matching logic is implemented.
LocationImportResponse.title: LocationImportResponse

LocationIngestRequest.description:
Ingest request — `delivery_type` applies to every net-new row in this
import (one Apricot sheet = one delivery type).
LocationIngestRequest.title: LocationIngestRequest

LocationRead-Input.description:
Read response model.

`has_future_route` is populated by the service layer (one batched query
against route_stops + route_groups), and `status` is derived from it +
in_roster. See LocationStatusEnum for the precedence rule.
LocationRead-Input.title: LocationRead

LocationRead-Output.description:
Read response model.

`has_future_route` is populated by the service layer (one batched query
against route_stops + route_groups), and `status` is derived from it +
in_roster. See LocationStatusEnum for the precedence rule.
LocationRead-Output.title: LocationRead

LocationStatusEnum.description:
Derived status surfaced on LocationRead. Not stored — computed from
Location.in_roster + whether the location appears in a present/future
route. Precedence: any present/future route → ACTIVE (regardless of
roster); otherwise in_roster → UNSCHEDULED; otherwise → INACTIVE.
LocationStatusEnum.title: LocationStatusEnum

LocationUpdate.description: Update request model with all fields optional
LocationUpdate.title: LocationUpdate

NetNewEntry.description: A row in the import that has no matching existing location.
NetNewEntry.title: NetNewEntry

NoteCreate.description: Create request model - chain_id and user_id set by the service
NoteCreate.title: NoteCreate

NoteListResponse.description: Response for GET notes - includes unread count
NoteListResponse.title: NoteListResponse

NotePermission.description: Controls who can read/write on a note chain
NotePermission.title: NotePermission

NoteUpdate.description: Update request model - only message can be edited
NoteUpdate.title: NoteUpdate

RefreshResponse.description: Refresh token response - only access token, refresh token is set as httpOnly cookie
RefreshResponse.title: RefreshResponse

RouteGenerationGroupInput.description: Input bundle for a single location group route generation.
RouteGenerationGroupInput.title: RouteGenerationGroupInput

RouteGenerationSettings.description:
Settings for route generation.

These are not persisted to the database; used as inputs to services.

route_duration_limit_minutes.description: Soft cap on total route duration (minutes). Routes exceeding this incur an optimization penalty to spread deliveries more evenly.
route_duration_limit_minutes.title: Route Duration Limit Minutes

RouteGenerationSettings.title: RouteGenerationSettings

RoutePatchRequest.description:
Request body for PATCH /routes/{route_id}.

All fields are optional - only provided fields will be updated.
If location_ids is provided, the route stops will be fully replaced
and the routing algorithm will be re-run to update polyline + mileage.
RoutePatchRequest.title: RoutePatchRequest

RouteWithDateRead.description:
Read response model for routes with drive date information.

drive_date is sourced from RouteGroup.drive_date via the route_group
relationship.
RouteWithDateRead.title: RouteWithDateRead

StaleEntry.description:
An existing location not present in the import; would be set
in_roster=False on ingest.
StaleEntry.title: StaleEntry

SuggestedDriverResponse.description:
A driver suggested for a route (most familiar with its locations from
past completed deliveries).
SuggestedDriverResponse.title: SuggestedDriverResponse

ValidatedLocationImportEntry.description: LocationImportEntry with required fields guaranteed non-None after validation.
ValidatedLocationImportEntry.title: ValidatedLocationImportEntry

post.description:
Register a new driver in our backend, creates a User and Driver object, returns DriverRead
NOTE: This does not create a firebase user, ie the User is in a hanging state
We need to do this so that we can implement our invite only system
Creates Firebase user and attaches to hanging state user in our local db, returns DriverRegisterResponse

get.description: Get a single driver by ID

delete.description: Delete a monthly driver history entry.

get.description:
Get driver history with optional year and month.
Rules:
- No year, no month: return all histories
- Year only: return all months for that year
- Year + month: return specific month
- Month without year: 400 error

patch.description:
Updates driver history
Rules:
- Driver history must exist with (driver_id, year, month)

post.description:
Creates new driver history
Rules:
- Driver must exist with driver_id
- Must be unique: (driver_id, year, month)

get.description: Get lifetime and current year KM summary for a driver

Export history for all drivers for a specific year. Includes data from that year and the previous year.

- driver_id: Must be "all"
- year: The year to export data for
Get all jobs

post.description: Persist net-new locations and archive stale ones.

Review a pending location import: validate rows and (eventually) describe how
the import would affect existing locations (net_new / stale / changed).
Requires a column_map JSON string mapping system field names to file headers.

Side effect: the submitted column_map is persisted to system_settings so it
becomes the default mapping on the next import.

get.description:
Get routes with pagination and optional filtering for unassigned routes and date range.
Returns routes with their drive dates - routes can appear multiple times for different dates.
When unassigned_only is False, returns all routes (no assignment filter).
When unassigned_only is True, returns only routes that are unassigned for the given route group.

Requires a driver or admin caller.
Admins may scope to any driver via driver_id (or omit it for all routes).
Drivers are always scoped to their own routes: omitting driver_id returns
their own routes, and requesting another driver's is rejected.

parameters.description: If true, only return unassigned routes. If false, return all routes regardless of assignment status.
parameters.name: unassigned_only

schema.description: If true, only return unassigned routes. If false, return all routes regardless of assignment status.
schema.title: Unassigned Only

parameters.description: Filter route groups from this date
parameters.name: start_date

schema.description: Filter route groups from this date
schema.title: Start Date

parameters.description: Filter route groups until this date
parameters.name: end_date

schema.description: Filter route groups until this date
schema.title: End Date

parameters.description: Page number (1-indexed)
parameters.name: page

schema.description: Page number (1-indexed)
schema.title: Page

parameters.description: Number of items per page
parameters.name: page_size

schema.description: Number of items per page
schema.title: Page Size

parameters.description: Filter routes by assigned driver. Admins may pass any driver_id, or omit it to get all routes. Drivers are always scoped to themselves: omitting it returns their own routes, and passing another driver's id is rejected with 403.
parameters.name: driver_id

schema.description: Filter routes by assigned driver. Admins may pass any driver_id, or omit it to get all routes. Drivers are always scoped to themselves: omitting it returns their own routes, and passing another driver's id is rejected with 403.
schema.title: Driver Id

delete.description:
Delete a route by its unique identifier.

Parameters:
    route_id (UUID): The unique identifier of the route to delete.
    session (AsyncSession): The database session dependency.
    _ (bool): Indicates that the user is authenticated as a driver (injected by dependency).

Returns:
    None. Responds with HTTP 204 No Content on successful deletion.

get.description:
Get a route by its unique identifier.

Parameters:
    route_id (UUID): The unique identifier of the route to GET.
    session (AsyncSession): The database session dependency.

Returns:
    None. Responds with HTTP 200 OK on successful get.

patch.description:
Update a route's metadata (name, notes) and/or its stop order/locations.

If location_ids is provided in the request body:
- The route's stops are fully replaced with the new ordered list.
- The routing algorithm is re-run to compute the new polyline and mileage.
- This will affect ALL route groups that share this route.

If only name/notes are provided, stops and mileage are left unchanged.

get.description:
Generate a Google Maps directions URL for a route.

Takes a route ID, looks up its stops in order, and builds a Google Maps
directions link starting from the warehouse and visiting each stop.

Parameters:
    route_id (UUID): The unique identifier of the route.
    session (AsyncSession): The database session dependency.

Returns:
    The Google Maps directions URL as a plain string.

Suggest a driver to assign to a route: the active driver most familiar
with the route's locations (by past completed deliveries), excluding
drivers already assigned within the given route group.

Parameters:
    route_id (UUID): The route to suggest a driver for.
    route_group_id (UUID): The route group the assignment is within.
    session (AsyncSession): The database session dependency.

Returns:
    The suggested driver, or null if there's no candidate.

parameters.description: Route group the route is being assigned within
parameters.name: route_group_id

schema.description: Route group the route is being assigned within
schema.title: Route Group Id

get.description: Return the singleton system settings row, or null if none has been created.

delete.description: Delete a file given the filename
